docs.title: 🍳 Welcome to My Recipe Bank
docs.text: Welcome to my personal digital cookbook! This project started as a way to expand my cooking repertoire beyond my usual staples, tracking everything from traditional Zimbabwean comfort foods like Sadza to global flavors from the Middle East and Europe.

docs.title: 💻 About This Project
docs.text: I wanted a way to document my recipes. Now with my recipes documented I don't struggle to pick what I'm cooking during the week and when I go for a food shop I have the ingredients list ready to go.

docs.title: ⭐ Featured Recipes
docs.text:
Not sure what to cook tonight? Here are a few of my absolute favorites:

  • Alfredo
  • Sadza neNyama
  • Butter Chicken
  • Creamy Tuscan Pasta
  • Egg Fried Rice

Tip: Use the search bar in the top left to quickly find a recipe by ingredient (e.g., type "beef" or "eggs").

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text: Quick, high-heat cooking with deeply savory, sweet, and umami-rich sauces. These dishes are faster, cheaper, and often better than takeout.

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• Mise en Place: Stir-frying happens incredibly fast (usually under 5 minutes). You must have all your veggies chopped, meat sliced, and sauce mixed before you turn on the stove.
  • Velveting Meat: Want that incredibly tender, melt-in-your-mouth meat you get at restaurants? Toss your sliced raw beef or chicken in 1 tbsp of soy sauce and 1 tbsp of cornstarch. Let it sit for 15 minutes before frying.
  • Don't Crowd the Pan: If you put too much meat or veg in the pan at once, the temperature drops and your food will boil/steam instead of searing. Cook in batches if necessary!
  • Aromatics First: Always start by frying your garlic, ginger, and scallions (green onions) for just 30 seconds to flavor the oil.

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text: No recipes added yet.

docs.title: 📖 overview
docs.text: A brief summary of this category. (e.g., "Pasta is my go-to for busy weeknights. Here is a collection of quick, creamy, and tomato-based pasta dishes.")

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
(Use this section for rules of thumb that apply to all recipes in this folder, so you don't have to rewrite them every time!)

  • Golden Rule 1: (e.g., Always salt your pasta water until it tastes like the sea.)
  • Golden Rule 2: (e.g., Reserve half a cup of pasta water to thicken your sauces.)
  • Basic Ratio: (e.g., 1 cup of rice to 2 cups of water.)

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text:
(Keep a running list of links to the recipes in this folder here. Since these are in the same folder, you just need the file name!)

No recipes added yet.

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text: These dishes are rich and hearty. Usually a quick prep and cooking time

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• The Sea Water Rule: Always salt your pasta water heavily until it tastes like the sea. This is your only chance to season the actual noodle.
  • Liquid Gold: Always reserve about ½ cup of the starchy pasta water before draining. Splashing a little into your sauce at the end binds the fat and water together, creating a glossy, restaurant-quality sauce.
  • Al Dente: Cook the pasta 1-2 minutes less than the box instructions. Drain it, put it directly into your simmering sauce, and let it finish cooking there so it absorbs the flavor.

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text:
• Creamy Tuscan Pasta

docs.title: Carbonara
docs.text:
A Roman classic made with eggs, hard cheese cured pork and black pepper. No cream needed!

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Spaghetti | 300g
  • Pancetta or Bacon | 150g (diced)
  • Eggs | 3-4 yolks
  • Parmesan or Pecorino cheese | 3/4 Cup (grated)
  • Black pepper | 1 Teaspoon (coarsely ground)

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Crisp: Sauté diced pancetta in a large cold skillet over medium heat until crispy. Keep some of the oil. Turn off the heat.
  2. Mix: In a bowl, whisk the egg yolks, grated cheese, and black pepper until well combined add some of that oil from before.
  3. Boil: Cook pasta in boiling salted water. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta water, then drain.
  4. Emulsify: Add hot pasta to the pancetta skillet (off the heat). Quickly pour in the egg mixture, tossing vigorously. Add pasta water a splash at a time until a glossy sauce forms. Toss in the meat

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Heat Warning: Never add the egg mixture while the pan is over a flame, or you will end up with scrambled eggs.

docs.title: Creamy Tuscan Pasta
docs.text:
Sun-dried tomatoes, parmesan, and cream come together in a silky sauce that makes this pasta feel like a proper Italian restaurant dish — serve it with garlic bread for the full experience.

Prep time: 15 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Spaghetti | 3/4 packet
  • Chicken Thighs | 450g
  • Butter | 1 tbsp
  • Garlic | 3 cloves
  • Tomato Puree | 1 tbsp
  • Chicken Stock | 1/3 cup
  • Heavy Cream | 2/3 cup
  • Sun Dried Tomatoes | 90g
  • Parmesan | 90g
  • Baby Spinach | 2 tbsp
  • Fresh Basil | 2 handfuls

Sauce / Spices

  • Smoked Paprika
  • Oregano
  • Onion Powder
  • Salt
  • Black Pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Mince the garlic, finely chop basil, grate the parmesan
  2. Cook the chicken: Marinate the thighs with the specified spices and brown it on both sides in olive oil - let it rest after cooking before cutting it
  3. Make the pasta: Bring a pot of salty water to a boil, toss in the pasta and cook for 1 less minute than the package instructions
  4. Make the sauce:
    • In the same pan melt the butter, saute the garlic
    • fry tomato puree
    • add the stock and cream
    • add the sundried tomatoes, basil and parmesan
    • stir in the spinach
    • toss in pasta and chicken
  5. Serve: Serve hot with garlic bread if youre feeling fancy and garnish with more cheese and any leftover basil

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Storage: 2 days in the fridge?
  • Substitutions: Don't have basil or olive? It's not that deep, skip the basil and use any oil you have
  • For the stock: I use stock cubes and dissolve it in some boiling water

docs.title: Fettuccine Alfredo
docs.text:
Rich, creamy, and indulgent. A simple yet luxurious Italian-American classic.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Fettuccine pasta | 300g
  • Heavy cream | 1 Cup
  • Unsalted butter | 1/3 Cup
  • Parmesan cheese | 1 Cup (freshly grated)
  • Garlic | 2 Cloves (minced)
  • Chicken thighs | 450g

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Thyme

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Boil: Cook fettuccine in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain, saving a little pasta water.
  2. Protein: Salt and pepper the chicken and cook in oil till golden brown. Let it rest before cutting it into bit sized pieces
  3. Melt: In a large skillet over medium-low heat, melt the butter. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
  4. Cream: Whisk in the heavy cream and let it simmer gently for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
  5. Cheese: Reduce heat to low. Gradually whisk in the parmesan cheese until completely smooth. Add the chicken and toss in the pasta to coat.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Mushroom Alfredo: Add mushrooms and cook until golden brown before adding the garlic
  • Consistency: If the sauce is too thick, use 1-2 tablespoons of the reserved pasta water to thin it out.
  • Flavor Boost: Add a pinch of thyme for extra depth.

docs.title: Mac & Cheese
docs.text:
Mac and cheese is the ultimate culinary hug combining American, Italian and French culinary knowledge into a single beloved comfort food.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Elbow macaroni | 300g
  • Cheddar cheese | 2 cups
  • Milk | 2 cups
  • Flour | 2 tbsp
  • Butter | 2 tbsp
  • Chicken thighs | 450g

Sauce / Spices

  • Garlic powder
  • Paprika
  • Salt
  • Black Pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Boil: Cook the macaroni in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain and set aside. (keep some pasta water)
  2. The Roux: In the same pot, melt butter over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute until it smells slightly nutty but hasn't browned.
  3. The Base: Gradually whisk in the milk. Simmer for 3–5 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon.
  4. The Melt: Turn off the heat. Stir in the garlic powder, paprika, and 1.5 cups of the cheese until completely smooth and velvety.
  5. The Finish: Fold the cooked pasta back into the sauce. If it's too thick, add a splash of milk/pasta water. Top with the remaining cheese and serve immediately.
  6. Pan-fry some chicken thighs and toss in a buffalo sauce (butter + hot sauce + garlic powder)

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Variations: Add other types of cheese; parmesan, gouda, whatever tickles your fancy.

docs.title: Marinara Pasta
docs.text:
A classic, comforting dish of pasta tossed in a rich, garlic-infused tomato sauce.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Spaghetti | 3/4 packet
  • Crushed canned tomatoes | 1 can
  • Garlic | 4 cloves
  • Onion | 1 small (optional)
  • Fresh basil | a handful
  • Protein (chicken thighs, beef) | 450g
  • Stock cube

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Red pepper flakes
  • Oregano
  • Bay Leaves

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Dice the onion and mince the garlic
  2. Boil: Cook pasta in a large pot of salted boiling water to 1 minute before al dente. Reserve some pasta water, then drain.
  3. Protein: Salt and pepper the chicken and cook in oil till golden brown. Let it rest before cutting it into bit sized pieces
  4. Aromatics: Heat some oil in a pan over medium heat. Cook onions till translucent then add the minced garlic and chili flakes and sauté until fragrant
  5. Simmer: Pour in the crushed tomatoes, salt, pepper, some oregano and a bay leaf(to do whatever it does). Add a half cup of stock stir and Simmer on low heat for 15 minutes to thicken.
  6. Toss: Stir in the fresh basil and drained pasta. Add a splash of pasta water if the sauce needs loosening. Toss until pasta is coated
  7. Plating: Garnish the dish with some shredded cheese and or fresh herbs like cilantro, parsley and the likes

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Substitution: Don't have oregano? Just add some generic Italian herbs/seasoning. Don't have that either? It's not that deep.
  • Variations: Using meatballs as the protein turns this into spaghetti and meatballs.

docs.title: Mushroom Alfredo
docs.text:
An earthy, savory twist on the classic Alfredo, loaded with golden-brown mushrooms.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Fettuccine pasta | 300g
  • Button mushrooms | 250g
  • Heavy cream | 1 Cup
  • Unsalted butter | 1/3 Cup
  • Parmesan cheese | 1 Cup (freshly grated)
  • Garlic | 2 Cloves (minced)
  • Chicken thighs | 450g

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Thyme

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Boil: Cook fettuccine in salted boiling water until al dente. Drain, saving a little pasta water.
  2. Protein: Salt and pepper the chicken and cook in oil till golden brown. Let it rest before cutting it into bit sized pieces
  3. Melt: In a large skillet over medium-low heat, melt the butter. Cook mushrooms until golden brown, add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
  4. Cream: Whisk in the heavy cream and let it simmer gently for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
  5. Cheese: Reduce heat to low. Gradually whisk in the parmesan cheese until completely smooth. Add the chicken and toss in the pasta to coat.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Consistency: If the sauce is too thick, use 1-2 tablespoons of the reserved pasta water to thin it out.
  • Flavor Boost: Add a pinch of thyme for extra depth.

docs.title: Spaghetti Bolognese
docs.text:
A deeply savory, slow-simmered Italian meat sauce served over long pasta

Prep time: 15 mins | Cook time: 45 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Spaghetti | 300g
  • Ground beef | 400g
  • Crushed tomatoes | 1 Can (400g)
  • Onion, Carrot, Celery | 1/2 cup each (finely diced)
  • Garlic | 3 Cloves (minced)
  • Tomato paste | 1 Tablespoon
  • Beef stock | 1 cube

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Soffritto: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion, carrot, and celery for 5-7 minutes until soft. Add garlic.
  2. Brown: Add the ground beef, breaking it apart with a spoon. Cook until fully browned.
  3. Simmer: Stir in tomato paste, then add crushed tomatoes and beef stock .Lower heat, cover, and gently simmer for at least 30 minutes.
  4. Serve: Cook spaghetti until al dente. Toss the pasta directly into the rich meat sauce before serving.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Simmer Time: Bolognese gets better the longer it cooks. If you have time, let it simmer on low for up to 2 hours.
  • Feeling fancy: Reduce red wine before adding the tomatoes and stock then simmer
  • Variation: A splash of milk balances the acidity of the tomatoes and helps create a rich, silky sauce.

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text: The ultimate comfort carb. Very versatile and keep well. These things would survive an apocalypse

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• Know Your Spuds: * Use Starchy potatoes (like Russets/Baking potatoes) for fluffy mashed potatoes or fries. Use Waxy potatoes (like red potatoes or baby potatoes) for stews and curries, as they won't disintegrate into mush when boiled.
  • The Crispy Secret: For the crispiest roasted potatoes, parboil them in heavily salted water with a pinch of baking soda for 10 minutes first. Drain them, rough them up by shaking them in the pot, and then roast them in hot oil.

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text: A good go to fr.

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• Wash Your Rice: Always rinse raw rice under cold water 3-4 times until the water runs clear. This removes surface starch and prevents it from becoming gummy and sticky.
  • The Golden Ratio: For standard long-grain white rice, use a 1 to 1.5 ratio (1 cup of rice to 2 cups of water) or use the finger knuckle trick.
  • Patience Pays Off: When the cooking time is up, turn off the heat but do not open the lid. Let it sit and steam in its own residual heat for 10 minutes before fluffing with a fork.
  • Fried Rice Secret: Never use freshly cooked, hot rice for fried rice. It will turn to mush. Always use day-old, cold rice straight from the fridge.

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text: • Egg Fried Rice

docs.title: 📖 Chicken and rice
docs.text:
Chicken and rice is the ultimate "standalone" dinner. By mastering a few flavorful bases, protein glazes, and fresh sides, you can create a different restaurant-quality meal every night of the week.

"How it works" This is your Build-Your-Own dinner dashboard. Pick one item from each column in the table below. Each link will take you to the specific recipe for that component.

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• The Rice Ratio: 1 cup rice to 2 cups liquid (Stock is always better than water!).
  • The Rest: Let the chicken rest for 5 mins before slicing to keep it juicy.
  • The Mix: Sautéing your rice in the "chicken fat" (if pan-frying) probably makes it taste better.

docs.title: 🛠️ The Build-Your-Own Meal
docs.text: 🍚 Step 1: Rice 🍗 Step 2: Chicken 🥗 Step 3: Salad Red Tomato Rice Honey Garlic Chicken Pico de Gallo Turmeric Yellow Rice Buffalo Chicken Creamy Coleslaw Garlic Butter Rice BBQ Glazed Chicken Classic Potato Salad

docs.title: 🌟 Recommended Combos
docs.text: • The Zing: Turmeric Rice + Buffalo Chicken + Pico de Gallo.

docs.title: Egg Fried Rice
docs.text:
One of the fastest, most satisfying meals you can make.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Eggs | 4
  • Rice | 1 cup (day old)
  • Onion | 1 medium
  • Garlic | 4 cloves
  • Bacon | 2-3 strips

Sauce / Spices

  • Soy Sauce
  • Sesame oil
  • White pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Chop the onions, mince the garlic.
  2. Protein and Eggs: Cook the bacon until golden brown then cut into little bits and set aside. Whisk the eggs in a bowl with some salt then scramble them in the same pan then set aside
  3. Aromatics: Cook the onions until their translucent then add the garlic for a further 30 seconds
  4. Combine: Once aromatics are fragrant add in the rice, break it up and fry it for a bit then add the eggs and bacon back in.
  5. Finish: Add in soy sauce, sesame oil and white pepper. Give it a final high-heat stir, and serve.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Substitutions: Don't have bacon? Use any protein of your liking or don't bother with it at all.
  • Make it Spicy: Add chili flakes during step 4 to turn up the heat.
  • Umami boost: Add a teaspoon of oyster sauce or MSG for restaurant-style depth

docs.title: BBQ Glazed Chicken
docs.text:
Smoky, bold, and slightly charred. This is the perfect way to get "grilled" flavors using just a standard kitchen pan.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Chicken thighs (skin-on preferred) | 500g

Sauce / Spices

  • Ketchup | 1/2 cup
  • Smoked paprika | 1 tsp
  • Brown sugar | 2 tbsp
  • Honey
  • Soy sauce
  • Worcestershire sauce
  • salt and pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Pat chicken dry. Mix the BBQ sauce, paprika, and sugar in a small bowl.
  2. Cook: Fry chicken skin-side down until crispy. Flip and cook until done.
  3. Combine: Combine the ingredients for the sauce. Simmer it to reduce and mix in the chicken.
  4. Serve: Rest for 2 minutes then slice and serve.

docs.title: Buffalo Chicken
docs.text:
A tangy, spicy classic. The butter mellows the heat of the vinegar-based hot sauce to create a rich, addictive coating.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 12 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Chicken thighs or breast (strips) | 1 lb
  • Oil | 1 tbsp

Sauce / Spices

  • Frank's RedHot (or similar) | 1/2 cup
  • Cold butter (cubed) | 1/4 cup
  • Garlic (minced) | 1/2 tsp
  • White Vinegar
  • Worcestershire sauce
  • Sugar/honey

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Slice the chicken into long strips.
  2. Cook: Pan-fry the chicken until crispy and golden.
  3. Combine: In a separate small pan (or the same pan after draining fat), whisk the hot sauce and garlic powder and a splash of vinegar. Add in some sugar/honey, Worchester sauce . Bring to a simmer, then whisk in cold butter one cube at a time until thick.
  4. Serve: Toss the cooked chicken in the sauce until fully coated.

docs.title: Creamy Coleslaw
docs.text:
Cool, crunchy, and refreshing. The perfect "fire extinguisher" for spicy buffalo or salty BBQ chicken.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 0 mins | Servings: 2-4

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Red and Green Cabbage (shredded) | 2 cups
  • Carrot (grated) | 1

Sauce / Spices

  • Mayonnaise | 1/2 cup
  • Apple cider vinegar | 1 tbsp
  • Sugar | 1 tsp
  • Black pepper
  • Salt

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Shred the cabbage and grate the carrot.
  2. Combine: Whisk the mayo, vinegar, and sugar in a large bowl. Fold in the vegetables.
  3. Serve: Best served chilled.

docs.title: Garlic Butter Rice
docs.text:
It’s simple, aromatic, and goes with absolutely everything.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Long grain white rice | 1 cup
  • Stock | 2 cups
  • Butter

Sauce / Spices

  • Garlic | 4 cloves
  • Fresh parsley | 1tbsp (chopped)

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Mince the garlic very finely.
  2. Cook: Melt 2 tbsp of butter in the pot. Sauté the garlic on low heat for 1-2 minutes—do not let it brown, or it will turn bitter.
  3. Combine: Stir in the rice, then add the stock. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer on low for 15 minutes.
  4. Serve: Stir in the final 1 tbsp of butter and the fresh parsley right before serving.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: Substitutions: Use water and a bouillon cube if you don't have liquid stock

docs.title: Honey Garlic Chicken
docs.text:
Sticky, sweet, and intensely savory. This glaze creates a restaurant-quality finish that clings perfectly to the chicken.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 12 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Chicken thighs (cubed) | 500g
  • Oil

Sauce / Spices

  • Honey | 3 tbsp
  • Soy sauce | 2 tbsp
  • Garlic (minced) | 3 cloves
  • Ginger (minced) | 1 tsp
  • Cornstarch

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and mince the garlic.
  2. Cook: Sear chicken (seasoned) in a hot pan with oil until golden and cooked through (8-10 mins).
  3. Combine: Lower the heat. Add the garlic and ginger. Pour in the honey, soy sauce. Stir constantly for 2-3 minutes until the sauce reduces into a thick, sticky glaze.
  4. Thicken: Pour in a cornstarch slurry to thicken the sauce if too watery
  5. Serve: Serve immediately while the glaze is glossy and hot.

docs.title: Pico de Gallo
docs.text:
A bright, acidic "salsa" that provides the perfect contrast to heavy, savory chicken and rice.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 0 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Tomatoes (seeded and diced) | 3 medium
  • Red/white onion (finely diced) | 1 small
  • Garlic | 2 cloves (minced)
  • Fresh cilantro (chopped) | 1/2 cup
  • Jalapeno | 1 large

Sauce / Spices

  • Lime (juiced) | 1
  • Salt | 1/2 tsp
  • Pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Dice everything into small, uniform cubes.
  2. Combine: Mix all ingredients in a glass bowl.
  3. Serve: Let it sit for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld before serving.

docs.title: Classic Potato Salad
docs.text:
Hearty and filling. This turns your chicken and rice into a massive feast that feels like a weekend celebration.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 15 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Potatoes (peeled and cubed) | 2 large
  • Red onion | diced
  • Cucumber | diced

Sauce / Spices

  • Mayonnaise | 1/2 cup
  • Yellow mustard | 1 tsp
  • Paprika | 1/2 tsp
  • Dill
  • Vinegar
  • Salt

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Boil potato cubes in salted water until tender (about 10-12 mins). Drain and cool.
  2. Combine: Mix mayo, mustard, vinegar, dill, and paprika, salt. Gently fold in the cooled potatoes.
  3. Serve: Top with an extra sprinkle of paprika for color.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Variations: Make a mixture of Greek yogurt and mayo for a lighter version.

docs.title: Red rice
docs.text:
The tomato paste caramelizes to create a deep, umami-rich base that makes the rice feel like a main event rather than just a side?

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Long grain white rice | 1 cup
  • Stock | 2 cups
  • Onions | medium (finely diced)
  • Garlic | 3 cloves

Sauce / Spices

  • Tomato paste | 2 tbsp
  • Paprika | 1 tsp
  • Salt-Pepper
  • Bay leaves
  • whatever spices you like tbh

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Rinse the rice until water runs clear. Dice the onion and mince the garlic.
  2. Cook: Sauté onions in a pot with oil until translucent, then add tomato paste and spices. Fry for 2 minutes until the paste turns a deep, dark red.
  3. Combine: Stir in the rice to coat every grain in the red oil. Pour in the stock, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 15 minutes.
  4. Serve: Let it rest with the lid on for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and serve.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: Substitutions: Use water and a bouillon cube if you don't have liquid stock

docs.title: Yellow rice
docs.text:
A vibrant, earthy, and aromatic rice that looks as good as it tastes.

Prep time: 5 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Long grain white rice | 1 cup
  • Stock | 2 cups
  • Onions | medium (finely diced)
  • Garlic | 3 cloves
  • Butter

Sauce / Spices

  • Turmeric
  • Bay leaves
  • Cumin
  • Salt
  • Pepper

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Rinse the rice until water runs clear. Dice the onion and mince the garlic.
  2. Cook: Sauté onions and garlic in the butter until translucent, then add the spices. Fry for 2 minutes until everything is well incorporated.
  3. Combine: Stir in the rice to coat every grain in the mixture. Pour in the stock, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 15 minutes.
  4. Serve: Let it rest with the lid on for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and serve.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: Substitutions: Use water and a bouillon cube if you don't have liquid stock

docs.title: 📖 Overview
docs.text: Thick comforting and filling.

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• The Cold Water Start: Always mix your initial meal with a little cold water to form a smooth paste before adding the boiling water. This guarantees you won't get lumps.
  • Let it Bubble (Kukwata): Don't rush the porridge stage. Let it simmer and bubble away for at least 15-20 minutes. Cooking the meal thoroughly at this stage is the secret to great-tasting sadza.
  • Kumona Sadza: When folding in the final meal to thicken it , use a sturdy wooden spoon and press against the sides of the pot to work out any dry pockets.

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text:
• Sadza
  • Sadza nemukaka
  • Sadza nemaveggie nenyama

docs.title: Sadza nemukaka
docs.text:
Quick and very comforting for the times when i can't be asked to cook for a long time

Prep time: 2 mins | Cook time: 35 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Sour milk (plain kefir) | 1 liter
  • Brown sugar | 1 tbsp

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Cook: Make some sadza
  2. Prepare the milk: In a bowl add some sour milk and the sugar and stir it well
  3. Combine: Add bite sized pieces of sadza to the bowl and enjoy

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Storage: Wrap the sadza in cling wrap and store in the fridge.

docs.title: Sadza neNyama
docs.text:
A hearty Zimbabwean classic — tender meat in a rich tomato broth served with sadza and greens for the ultimate home-cooked meal.

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 40 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Kale/Collard greens | 900g
  • Onion | 1 medium onion
  • Garlic | 4 cloves
  • Tomato paste | 2 tsp
  • Fresh/canned chopped tomatoes | x2
  • Stock | 1 cube
  • Chicken/Beef | 450g
  • Bisto/Cornstarch

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Paprika
  • Onion powder
  • Garlic powder

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Chop the onions, mince the garlic, chop the greens
  2. Make the soup: Season and brown the meat and set aside.
  3. Aromatics: Fry the onions, garlic and tomato paste then add the tomatoes and stock and let that simmer for a bit
  4. Combine: Mix in a cornstarch slurry to the soup and add the meat back in and let that simmer to get the meat tender
  5. Make the sadza While the soup simmers make sadza
  6. Fry the veg: Fry the veggies and salt them while they're still hot
  7. Serve: Serve the sadza and relish hot and enjoy

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • If using beef: Boil the meat with some spices to tenderize it before frying

docs.title: Basic Sadza
docs.text:
The foundational staple of Zimbabwean cuisine. Cooked low and slow until perfectly firm, ready to be paired with greens, meat, or sour milk.

Prep time: 2 mins | Cook time: 35 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Mealie meal | 1 Mug (plus extra for folding)
  • Boiling water | 4 Cups

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Boil about 4 cups of water in a kettle.
  2. Make the Base: In your pot, mix a little mealie meal with cold water to make a smooth paste. Pour in the boiling water while stirring constantly to make a thin porridge base.
  3. Simmer: Keep stirring until it begins to simmer (violently). Reduce the heat to medium-low, put the lid on, and let it simmer for about 15 minutes (kukwata).
  4. Fold (Kumona): Reduce the heat to low. Start adding dry mealie meal a little at a time, folding and combining it into the porridge with a wooden spoon until you reach the desired consistency (like firm mashed potatoes).
  5. Final Steam: Put the lid back on and let it cook on low for another 10 minutes to cook out the raw flour taste.
  6. Serve: Mix the sadza one more time. Use a serving spoon dipped in water or lightly brushed with oil to shape it and prevent sticking.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Storage: Once you make a mound, wrap it tightly in cling foil and store it in the fridge. It reheats wonderfully in the microwave.

docs.title: 📖 overview
docs.text: Slow-cooked, deeply comforting, and packed with flavor. Whether it's a rich beef gravy or a warmly spiced Indian curry, these dishes are perfect for cozy nights in and always taste even better the next day.

docs.title: 💡 General Tips & Ratios
docs.text:
• Brown the Meat First: Never skip the sear! Browning your chunks of beef or chicken in hot oil before adding your liquids creates a deep, savory crust (the Maillard reaction) that flavors the entire pot.
  • Deglaze the Pan: After searing meat or cooking down onions, pour a splash of broth or water into the hot pan and scrape up all those delicious brown bits stuck to the bottom. That is pure flavor!
  • Bloom Your Spices: For curries, cook your dry spices (like cumin, coriander, garamasala, and turmeric) in hot oil or butter for 30–60 seconds before adding your wet ingredients (like tomatoes or coconut milk). This "blooms" the spices, releasing their essential oils and making the dish infinitely more flavorful.
  • Low and Slow: Tough cuts of meat (like stewing beef or oxtail) need time to break down the connective tissue. Keep the heat low and let the pot gently simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours. Boiling it rapidly will actually make the meat tough and rubbery.

docs.title: 📜 Recipes in this Category
docs.text:
• Butter Chicken
  • Goulash

docs.title: Butter Chicken
docs.text:
Rich, creamy, and warmly spiced. Its my ultimate comfort curry that pairs perfectly with basmati rice or naan.

Prep time: 15 mins | Cook time: 20 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Chicken thigh | 500g
  • Butter | 2tbsp
  • Chopped tomatoes | 1 can
  • Heavy cream | 1/2 cup
  • Greek yogurt | 1/2 cup
  • Ginger | 1/2 tbsp
  • Garlic | 1/2 tbsp

Sauce / Spices

  • Garam masala | 1 tsp
  • Turmeric | 1/2 tsp
  • Chili powder | 1 tsp
  • Cilantro (garnish)

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Marinate: Toss chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic, garam masala, turmeric, and chili powder. Let sit for 30 mins (or overnight).
  2. Sear: Melt 1 tbsp of butter in a pan over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken in batches until golden. Remove and set aside (it doesn't need to be cooked through yet).
  3. Simmer: In the same pan, add the remaining butter and diced onion, more ginger-garlic and tomato paste. Stir in the seasoning from before then add the chopped tomatoes. Simmer for 5–8 minutes until the sauce thickens and darkens.
  4. Finish: Stir in the heavy cream (blend it at this point if you want) and return the chicken to the pan. Simmer for another 5 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce is glossy.
  5. Serve: Garnish with a swirl of cream or fresh cilantro.

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text: • Dessert: Use any leftover cream to make lemon pouset

docs.title: Goulash
docs.text:
A hearty soup. That goes well with rice. Kinda similar to a stew you'd make for Sadza nenyama just with potatoes and carrots added to it. A dish found all around the globe going by different names, in Hungary its goulash (Gulyás) in Zimbabwe its simply 'beef stew'

Prep time: 10 mins | Cook time: 60 mins | Servings: 3

docs.title: 🛒 Ingredients
docs.text:
Main

  • Onion | 1 medium onion
  • Garlic | 4 cloves
  • Tomato paste | 2 tsp
  • Potatoes
  • Carrots
  • Tomatoes (chopped) | 1 can
  • Stock | 2 cubes
  • Beef | 500g

Sauce / Spices

  • Salt
  • Pepper
  • Paprika
  • Bay Leaves

docs.title: 🍳 Instructions
docs.text:
1. Prep: Chop the onions, mince the garlic, cube the potatoes and carrots
  2. Brown the meat: Season and brown the meat. Add and fry the onions, garlic and tomato paste. Season with the spices above and add a bay leaf
  3. Make the soup: Add the tomatoes let that incorporate then add stock and let that simmer for 40 minutes
  4. Add vegetables: Add in the potatoes and carrots and simmer until their fork tender
  5. Garnish: Just before finishing toss in some chopped parsley if you're feeling fancy

docs.title: 💡 Notes & Variations
docs.text:
• Making it spicier: I like to add some chili peppers to the seasoning to give it a kick.
  • Variations: Green and red peppers are also welcome to be added in step 3.
